mismatches.sourceText: Upon my bed this is what I saw; there was a tree at the centre of the earth, and its height was great.

mismatches.activeText: The tree grew and became strong; its top reached to heaven, and it was visible to the ends of the whole earth.
mismatches.sourceText: The tree grew great and strong, its top reached to heaven, and it was visible to the ends of the whole earth.

mismatches.activeText: Its foliage was beautiful and its fruit abundant, and it provided food for all. The animals of the field found shade under it, the birds of the air nested in its branches, and from it all living beings were fed.
mismatches.sourceText: Its foliage was beautiful, its fruit abundant, and it provided food for all. The animals of the field found shade under it, the birds of the air nested in its branches, and from it all living beings were fed.

mismatches.activeText: I continued to look in the visions of my head as I lay in bed, and there was a holy watcher, coming down from heaven.
mismatches.sourceText: ‘I continued looking, in the visions of my head as I lay in bed, and there was a holy watcher, coming down from heaven.

mismatches.activeText: He cried aloud and said: ‘Cut down the tree and chop off its branches, strip off its foliage and scatter its fruit. Let the animals flee from under it and the birds from its branches.
mismatches.sourceText: He cried aloud and said: “Cut down the tree and chop off its branches, strip off its foliage and scatter its fruit. Let the animals flee from beneath it and the birds from its branches.

mismatches.activeText: The sentence is rendered by decree of the watchers, the decision is given by word of the holy ones, in order that all who live may know that the Most High is sovereign over the kingdom of mortals; he gives it to whom he will and sets over it the lowliest of human beings.’
mismatches.sourceText: The sentence is rendered by decree of the watchers, the decision is given by order of the holy ones, in order that all who live may know that the Most High is sovereign over the kingdom of mortals; he gives it to whom he will and sets over it the lowliest of human beings.”

mismatches.activeText: “This is the dream that I, King Nebuchadnezzar, saw. Now you, Belteshazzar, tell me the interpretation, since all the wise men of my kingdom are unable to tell me the interpretation; you are able, however, for you are endowed with a spirit of the holy gods.”
mismatches.sourceText: ‘This is the dream that I, King Nebuchadnezzar, saw. Now you, Belteshazzar, declare the interpretation, since all the wise men of my kingdom are unable to tell me the interpretation. You are able, however, for you are endowed with a spirit of the holy gods.’

mismatches.activeText: Then Daniel, who was called Belteshazzar, was severely distressed for a while, and his thoughts terrified him. The king said, “Belteshazzar, do not let the dream or the interpretation terrify you.” Belteshazzar answered, “My lord, may the dream be for those who hate you, and its interpretation for your enemies!
mismatches.sourceText: Daniel Interprets the Second Dream Then Daniel, who was called Belteshazzar, was severely distressed for a while. His thoughts terrified him. The king said, ‘Belteshazzar, do not let the dream or the interpretation terrify you.’ Belteshazzar answered, ‘My lord, may the dream be for those who hate you, and its interpretation for your enemies!

mismatches.activeText: The tree that you saw, which grew and became strong, so that its top reached to heaven and was visible to the end of the whole earth,
mismatches.sourceText: The tree that you saw, which grew great and strong, so that its top reached to heaven and was visible to the end of the whole earth,

mismatches.activeText: it is you, O king! You have grown and become strong. Your greatness has increased and reaches to heaven, and your sovereignty to the ends of the earth.
mismatches.sourceText: it is you, O king! You have grown great and strong. Your greatness has increased and reaches to heaven, and your sovereignty to the ends of the earth.

mismatches.activeText: And whereas the king saw a holy watcher coming down from heaven and saying, ‘Cut down the tree and destroy it, but leave its stump and roots in the ground, with a band of iron and bronze, in the tender grass of the field; and let him be bathed with the dew of heaven, and let his lot be with the animals of the field, until seven times pass over him’—

mismatches.activeText: this is the interpretation, O king: it is a decree of the Most High that has come upon my lord the king:
mismatches.sourceText: this is the interpretation, O king, and it is a decree of the Most High that has come upon my lord the king:

mismatches.activeText: you shall be driven away from human society, and your dwelling shall be with the wild animals. You shall be made to eat grass like oxen, and you shall be bathed with the dew of heaven, and seven times shall pass over you, until you have learned that the Most High has sovereignty over the kingdom of mortals and gives it to whom he will.
mismatches.sourceText: You shall be driven away from human society, and your dwelling shall be with the wild animals. You shall be made to eat grass like oxen, you shall be bathed with the dew of heaven, and seven times shall pass over you, until you have learned that the Most High has sovereignty over the kingdom of mortals, and gives it to whom he will.

mismatches.activeText: Therefore, O king, may my counsel be acceptable to you: atone for your sins with righteousness, and your iniquities with mercy to the oppressed, so that your prosperity may perhaps be prolonged.”
mismatches.sourceText: Therefore, O king, may my counsel be acceptable to you: atone for your sins with righteousness, and your iniquities with mercy to the oppressed, so that your prosperity may be prolonged.’

mismatches.activeText: All this came upon King Nebuchadnezzar.
mismatches.sourceText: Nebuchadnezzar’s Humiliation All this came upon King Nebuchadnezzar.

mismatches.activeText: and the king uttered these words: “Is this not magnificent Babylon, which I have built as a royal residence by my mighty power and for my glorious majesty?”
mismatches.sourceText: and the king said, ‘Is this not magnificent Babylon, which I have built as a royal capital by my mighty power and for my glorious majesty?’

mismatches.activeText: While the words were still in the king’s mouth, a voice came from heaven: “O King Nebuchadnezzar, to you it is declared: The kingdom has departed from you!
mismatches.sourceText: While the words were still in the king’s mouth, a voice came from heaven: ‘O King Nebuchadnezzar, to you it is declared: The kingdom has departed from you!

mismatches.activeText: You shall be driven away from human society, and your dwelling shall be with the wild animals. You shall be made to eat grass like oxen, and seven times shall pass over you, until you have learned that the Most High has sovereignty over the kingdom of mortals and gives it to whom he will.”
mismatches.sourceText: You shall be driven away from human society, and your dwelling shall be with the animals of the field. You shall be made to eat grass like oxen, and seven times shall pass over you, until you have learned that the Most High has sovereignty over the kingdom of mortals and gives it to whom he will.’

mismatches.activeText: When that period was over, I, Nebuchadnezzar, lifted my eyes to heaven, and my reason returned to me. I blessed the Most High, and praised and honored the one who lives forever. For his sovereignty is an everlasting sovereignty, and his kingdom endures from generation to generation.
mismatches.sourceText: Nebuchadnezzar Praises God When that period was over, I, Nebuchadnezzar, lifted my eyes to heaven, and my reason returned to me. I blessed the Most High, and praised and honoured the one who lives for ever. For his sovereignty is an everlasting sovereignty, and his kingdom endures from generation to generation.

mismatches.activeText: All the inhabitants of the earth are accounted as nothing, and he does what he wills with the host of heaven and the inhabitants of the earth. There is no one who can stay his hand or say to him, “What are you doing?”
mismatches.sourceText: All the inhabitants of the earth are accounted as nothing, and he does what he wills with the host of heaven and the inhabitants of the earth. There is no one who can stay his hand or say to him, ‘What are you doing?’

mismatches.activeText: At that time my reason returned to me; and my majesty and splendor were restored to me for the glory of my kingdom. My counselors and my lords sought me out, I was re-established in my kingdom, and still more greatness was added to me.
mismatches.sourceText: At that time my reason returned to me; and my majesty and splendour were restored to me for the glory of my kingdom. My counsellors and my lords sought me out, I was re-established over my kingdom, and still more greatness was added to me.

mismatches.activeText: Now I, Nebuchadnezzar, praise and extol and honor the King of heaven, for all his works are truth, and his ways are justice; and he is able to humble those who walk in pride.
mismatches.sourceText: Now I, Nebuchadnezzar, praise and extol and honour the King of heaven, for all his works are truth, and his ways are justice; and he is able to bring low those who walk in pride.

mismatches.activeText: King Belshazzar made a great festival for a thousand of his lords, and he was drinking wine in the presence of the thousand.
mismatches.sourceText: Belshazzar’s Feast 5

mismatches.activeText: Immediately the fingers of a human hand appeared and began writing on the plaster of the wall of the royal palace, next to the lampstand. The king was watching the hand as it wrote.
mismatches.sourceText: The Writing on the Wall Immediately the fingers of a human hand appeared and began writing on the plaster of the wall of the royal palace, next to the lampstand. The king was watching the hand as it wrote.

mismatches.activeText: The king cried aloud to bring in the enchanters, the Chaldeans, and the diviners; and the king said to the wise men of Babylon, “Whoever can read this writing and tell me its interpretation shall be clothed in purple, have a chain of gold around his neck, and rank third in the kingdom.”
mismatches.sourceText: The king cried aloud to bring in the enchanters, the Chaldeans, and the diviners; and the king said to the wise men of Babylon, ‘Whoever can read this writing and tell me its interpretation shall be clothed in purple, have a chain of gold around his neck, and rank third in the kingdom.’

mismatches.activeText: Then King Belshazzar became sufficiently terrified and his face turned pale, and his lords were perplexed.
mismatches.sourceText: Then King Belshazzar became greatly terrified and his face turned pale, and his lords were perplexed.

mismatches.activeText: The queen, when she heard the discussion of the king and his lords, came into the banqueting hall. The queen said, “O king, live forever! Do not let your thoughts terrify you or your face grow pale.
mismatches.sourceText: The queen, when she heard the discussion of the king and his lords, came into the banqueting-hall. The queen said, ‘O king, live for ever! Do not let your thoughts terrify you or your face grow pale.

mismatches.activeText: There is a man in your kingdom who is endowed with a spirit of the holy gods. In the days of your father he was found to have enlightenment, understanding, and wisdom like the wisdom of the gods. Your father, King Nebuchadnezzar, made him chief of the magicians, enchanters, Chaldeans, and diviners.
mismatches.sourceText: There is a man in your kingdom who is endowed with a spirit of the holy gods. In the days of your father he was found to have enlightenment, understanding, and wisdom like the wisdom of the gods. Your father, King Nebuchadnezzar, made him chief of the magicians, enchanters, Chaldeans, and diviners,

mismatches.activeText: Because an excellent spirit, knowledge, and understanding to interpret dreams, explain riddles, and solve problems were found in this Daniel, whom the king named Belteshazzar. Now let Daniel be called, and he will give the interpretation.”
mismatches.sourceText: because an excellent spirit, knowledge, and understanding to interpret dreams, explain riddles, and solve problems were found in this Daniel, whom the king named Belteshazzar. Now let Daniel be called, and he will give the interpretation.’

mismatches.activeText: Then Daniel was brought in before the king. The king said to Daniel, “Are you that Daniel, one of the exiles of Judah, whom my father the king brought from Judah?
mismatches.sourceText: The Writing on the Wall Interpreted Then Daniel was brought in before the king. The king said to Daniel, ‘So you are Daniel, one of the exiles of Judah, whom my father the king brought from Judah?

mismatches.activeText: Now the wise men and the enchanters have been brought in before me to read this writing and tell me its interpretation, but they were not able to give the interpretation of the matter.
mismatches.sourceText: Now the wise men, the enchanters, have been brought in before me to read this writing and tell me its interpretation, but they were not able to give the interpretation of the matter.

mismatches.activeText: But I have heard that you can give interpretations and solve problems. Now if you are able to read the writing and tell me its interpretation, you shall be clothed in purple, have a chain of gold around your neck, and rank third in the kingdom.”
mismatches.sourceText: But I have heard that you can give interpretations and solve problems. Now if you are able to read the writing and tell me its interpretation, you shall be clothed in purple, have a chain of gold around your neck, and rank third in the kingdom.’

mismatches.activeText: Then Daniel answered in the presence of the king, “Keep your gifts for yourself, and give your rewards to another! Nevertheless I will read the writing to the king and let him know the interpretation.
mismatches.sourceText: Then Daniel answered in the presence of the king, ‘Let your gifts be for yourself, or give your rewards to someone else! Nevertheless, I will read the writing to the king and let him know the interpretation.

mismatches.activeText: And because of the greatness that he gave him, all peoples, nations, and languages trembled and feared before him. He killed those he wanted to kill, and kept alive those he wanted to keep alive; he honored those he wanted to honor, and humbled those he wanted to humble.
mismatches.sourceText: And because of the greatness that he gave him, all peoples, nations, and languages trembled and feared before him. He killed those he wanted to kill, kept alive those he wanted to keep alive, honoured those he wanted to honour, and degraded those he wanted to degrade.

mismatches.activeText: But when his heart was lifted up and his spirit was hardened so that he acted proudly, he was deposed from his kingly throne, and his glory was taken from him.
mismatches.sourceText: But when his heart was lifted up and his spirit was hardened so that he acted proudly, he was deposed from his kingly throne, and his glory was stripped from him.

mismatches.activeText: He was driven from human society, and his mind was made like that of an animal. His dwelling was with the wild asses, he was fed grass like oxen, and his body was bathed with the dew of heaven, until he learned that the Most High God has sovereignty over the kingdom of mortals, and sets over it whomever he will.

mismatches.activeText: You have exalted yourself against the Lord of heaven! The vessels of his house have been brought in before you, and you and your lords, your wives and your concubines have been drinking wine from them. You have praised the gods of silver and gold, of bronze, iron, wood, and stone, which do not see or hear or know; but the God in whose hand is your very breath, and to whom belong all your ways, you have not honored.
mismatches.sourceText: You have exalted yourself against the Lord of heaven! The vessels of his temple have been brought in before you, and you and your lords, your wives and your concubines have been drinking wine from them. You have praised the gods of silver and gold, of bronze, iron, wood, and stone, which do not see or hear or know; but the God in whose power is your very breath, and to whom belong all your ways, you have not honoured.

mismatches.activeText: “So from his presence the hand was sent and this writing was inscribed.
mismatches.sourceText: ‘So from his presence the hand was sent and this writing was inscribed.

mismatches.activeText: And this is the writing that was inscribed: mene, mene, tekel, and parsin.
mismatches.sourceText: And this is the writing that was inscribed: mene

mismatches.activeText: This is the interpretation of the matter: mene, God has numbered the days of your kingdom and brought it to an end;
mismatches.sourceText: This is the interpretation of the matter: mene

mismatches.activeText: tekel, you have been weighed on the scales and found wanting;
mismatches.sourceText: tekel

mismatches.activeText: peres, your kingdom is divided and given to the Medes and Persians.”
mismatches.sourceText: peres

mismatches.activeText: It pleased Darius to set over the kingdom one hundred twenty satraps, stationed throughout the whole kingdom,
mismatches.sourceText: The Plot against Daniel 6

mismatches.activeText: and over them three presidents, including Daniel; to these the satraps were to give account, so that the king might suffer no loss.
mismatches.sourceText: and over them three presidents, including Daniel; to these the satraps gave account, so that the king might suffer no loss.

mismatches.activeText: So the presidents and the satraps tried to find grounds for complaint against Daniel in connection with the kingdom. But they could find no grounds for complaint or any corruption, because he was faithful, and no negligence or corruption was found in him.
mismatches.sourceText: So the presidents and the satraps tried to find grounds for complaint against Daniel in connection with the kingdom. But they could find no grounds for complaint or any corruption, because he was faithful, and no negligence or corruption could be found in him.

mismatches.activeText: The men said, “We shall not find any ground for complaint against this Daniel unless we find it in connection with the law of his God.”
mismatches.sourceText: The men said, ‘We shall not find any ground for complaint against this Daniel unless we find it in connection with the law of his God.’

mismatches.activeText: So the presidents and satraps conspired and came to the king and said to him, “O King Darius, live forever!
mismatches.sourceText: So the presidents and satraps conspired and came to the king and said to him, ‘O King Darius, live for ever!

mismatches.activeText: All the presidents of the kingdom, the prefects and the satraps, the counselors and the governors are agreed that the king should establish an ordinance and enforce an interdict, that whoever prays to anyone, divine or human, for thirty days, except to you, O king, shall be thrown into a den of lions.
mismatches.sourceText: All the presidents of the kingdom, the prefects and the satraps, the counsellors and the governors, are agreed that the king should establish an ordinance and enforce an interdict, that whoever prays to anyone, divine or human, for thirty days, except to you, O king, shall be thrown into a den of lions.

mismatches.activeText: Now, O king, establish the interdict and sign the document, so that it cannot be changed, according to the law of the Medes and the Persians, which cannot be revoked.”
mismatches.sourceText: Now, O king, establish the interdict and sign the document, so that it cannot be changed, according to the law of the Medes and the Persians, which cannot be revoked.’

mismatches.activeText: Although Daniel knew that the document had been signed, he continued to go to his house, which had windows in its upper room open toward Jerusalem, and to get down on his knees three times a day to pray to his God and praise him, just as he had done previously.
mismatches.sourceText: Daniel in the Lions’ Den Although Daniel knew that the document had been signed, he continued to go to his house, which had windows in its upper room open towards Jerusalem, and to get down on his knees three times a day to pray to his God and praise him, just as he had done previously.

mismatches.activeText: Then they approached the king and said concerning the interdict, “O king! Did you not sign an interdict, that anyone who prays to anyone, divine or human, within thirty days, except to you, O king, shall be thrown into a den of lions?” The king answered, “The thing is fixed, according to the law of the Medes and Persians, which cannot be revoked.”
mismatches.sourceText: Then they approached the king and said concerning the interdict, ‘O king! Did you not sign an interdict, that anyone who prays to anyone, divine or human, within thirty days except to you, O king, shall be thrown into a den of lions?’ The king answered, ‘The thing stands fast, according to the law of the Medes and Persians, which cannot be revoked.’

mismatches.activeText: Then they responded to the king, “Daniel, one of the exiles from Judah, pays no attention to you, O king, or to the interdict you have signed, but he is saying his prayers three times a day.”
mismatches.sourceText: Then they responded to the king, ‘Daniel, one of the exiles from Judah, pays no attention to you, O king, or to the interdict you have signed, but he is saying his prayers three times a day.’

mismatches.activeText: When the king heard this, he was much distressed and set his mind to deliver Daniel; and until the sun went down he made every effort to rescue him.
mismatches.sourceText: When the king heard the charge, he was very much distressed. He was determined to save Daniel, and until the sun went down he made every effort to rescue him.

mismatches.activeText: Then the conspirators came to the king and said to him, “Know, O king, that it is a law of the Medes and Persians that no interdict or ordinance that the king establishes can be changed.”
mismatches.sourceText: Then the conspirators came to the king and said to him, ‘Know, O king, that it is a law of the Medes and Persians that no interdict or ordinance that the king establishes can be changed.’

mismatches.activeText: Then the king gave the command, and Daniel was brought and thrown into the den of lions. The king said to Daniel, “May your God, whom you faithfully serve, deliver you!”
mismatches.sourceText: Then the king gave the command, and Daniel was brought and thrown into the den of lions. The king said to Daniel, ‘May your God, whom you faithfully serve, deliver you!’

mismatches.activeText: Then, at break of day, the king got up and hurried to the den of lions.
mismatches.sourceText: Daniel Saved from the Lions Then, at break of day, the king got up and hurried to the den of lions.

mismatches.activeText: When he came near the den where Daniel was, he cried out anxiously to Daniel, “O Daniel, servant of the living God, has your God whom you faithfully serve been able to deliver you from the lions?”
mismatches.sourceText: When he came near the den where Daniel was, he cried out anxiously to Daniel, ‘O Daniel, servant of the living God, has your God whom you faithfully serve been able to deliver you from the lions?’

mismatches.activeText: Daniel then said to the king, “O king, live forever!
mismatches.sourceText: Daniel then said to the king, ‘O king, live for ever!

mismatches.activeText: My God sent his angel and shut the lions’ mouths so that they would not hurt me, because I was found blameless before him; and also before you, O king, I have done no wrong.”
mismatches.sourceText: My God sent his angel and shut the lions’ mouths so that they would not hurt me, because I was found blameless before him; and also before you, O king, I have done no wrong.’

mismatches.activeText: The king gave a command, and those who had accused Daniel were brought and thrown into the den of lions, they, their children, and their wives. Before they reached the bottom of the den the lions overpowered them and broke all their bones in pieces.
mismatches.sourceText: The king gave a command, and those who had accused Daniel were brought and thrown into the den of lions—they, their children, and their wives. Before they reached the bottom of the den the lions overpowered them and broke all their bones in pieces.

mismatches.activeText: Then King Darius wrote to all peoples and nations of every language throughout the whole world: “May you have abundant prosperity!
mismatches.sourceText: Then King Darius wrote to all peoples and nations of every language throughout the whole world: ‘May you have abundant prosperity!

mismatches.activeText: I make a decree, that in all my royal dominion people should tremble and fear before the God of Daniel: For he is the living God, enduring forever. His kingdom shall never be destroyed, and his dominion has no end.
mismatches.sourceText: I make a decree, that in all my royal dominion people should tremble and fear before the God of Daniel: For he is the living God, enduring for ever. His kingdom shall never be destroyed, and his dominion has no end.

mismatches.activeText: He delivers and rescues, he works signs and wonders in heaven and on earth; for he has saved Daniel from the power of the lions.”
mismatches.sourceText: He delivers and rescues, he works signs and wonders in heaven and on earth; for he has saved Daniel from the power of the lions.’

mismatches.activeText: In the first year of King Belshazzar of Babylon, Daniel had a dream and visions of his head as he lay in bed. Then he wrote down the dream:
mismatches.sourceText: Visions of the Four Beasts 7

mismatches.activeText: Another beast appeared, a second one, that looked like a bear. It was raised up on one side, had three tusks in its mouth among its teeth and was told, “Arise, devour many bodies!”
mismatches.sourceText: Another beast appeared, a second one, that looked like a bear. It was raised up on one side, had three tusks in its mouth among its teeth and was told, ‘Arise, devour many bodies!’

mismatches.activeText: After this I saw in the visions by night a fourth beast, terrifying and extraordinarily strong. It had great iron teeth and was devouring, breaking in pieces, and stamping what was left with its feet. It was different from all the beasts that preceded it, and it had ten horns.
mismatches.sourceText: After this I saw in the visions by night a fourth beast, terrifying and dreadful and exceedingly strong. It had great iron teeth and was devouring, breaking in pieces, and stamping what was left with its feet. It was different from all the beasts that preceded it, and it had ten horns.

mismatches.activeText: As I watched, thrones were set in place, and an Ancient One took his throne, his clothing was white as snow, and the hair of his head like pure wool; his throne was fiery flames, and its wheels were burning fire.
mismatches.sourceText: Judgement before the Ancient One As I watched, thrones were set in place, and an Ancient One took his throne; his clothing was white as snow, and the hair of his head like pure wool; his throne was fiery flames, and its wheels were burning fire.

mismatches.activeText: A stream of fire issued and flowed out from his presence. A thousand thousands served him, and ten thousand times ten thousand stood attending him. The court sat in judgment, and the books were opened.
mismatches.sourceText: A stream of fire issued and flowed out from his presence. A thousand thousand served him, and ten thousand times ten thousand stood attending him. The court sat in judgement, and the books were opened.

mismatches.activeText: As for me, Daniel, my spirit was troubled within me, and the visions of my head terrified me.
mismatches.sourceText: Daniel’s Visions Interpreted As for me, Daniel, my spirit was troubled within me, and the visions of my head terrified me.

mismatches.activeText: I approached one of those standing there and asked him the truth concerning all this. So he said that he would disclose to me the interpretation of the matter:
mismatches.sourceText: I approached one of the attendants to ask him the truth concerning all this. So he said that he would disclose to me the interpretation of the matter:

mismatches.activeText: “As for these four great beasts, four kings shall arise out of the earth.
mismatches.sourceText: ‘As for these four great beasts, four kings shall arise out of the earth.

mismatches.activeText: But the holy ones of the Most High shall receive the kingdom and possess the kingdom forever—forever and ever.”
mismatches.sourceText: But the holy ones of the Most High shall receive the kingdom and possess the kingdom for ever—for ever and ever.’

mismatches.activeText: and concerning the ten horns that were on its head, and concerning the other horn, which came up and to make room for which three of them fell out—the horn that had eyes and a mouth that spoke arrogantly, and that seemed greater than the others.

mismatches.activeText: until the Ancient One came; then judgment was given for the holy ones of the Most High, and the time arrived when the holy ones gained possession of the kingdom.
mismatches.sourceText: until the Ancient One came; then judgement was given for the holy ones of the Most High, and the time arrived when the holy ones gained possession of the kingdom.

mismatches.activeText: This is what he said: “As for the fourth beast, there shall be a fourth kingdom on earth that shall be different from all the other kingdoms; it shall devour the whole earth, and trample it down, and break it to pieces.
mismatches.sourceText: This is what he said: ‘As for the fourth beast, there shall be a fourth kingdom on earth that shall be different from all the other kingdoms; it shall devour the whole earth, and trample it down, and break it to pieces.

mismatches.activeText: Then the court shall sit in judgment, and his dominion shall be taken away, to be consumed and totally destroyed.
mismatches.sourceText: Then the court shall sit in judgement, and his dominion shall be taken away, to be consumed and totally destroyed.

mismatches.activeText: The kingship and dominion and the greatness of the kingdoms under the whole heaven shall be given to the people of the holy ones of the Most High; their kingdom shall be an everlasting kingdom, and all dominions shall serve and obey them.”
mismatches.sourceText: The kingship and dominion and the greatness of the kingdoms under the whole heaven shall be given to the people of the holy ones of the Most High; their kingdom shall be an everlasting kingdom, and all dominions shall serve and obey them.’

mismatches.activeText: In the third year of the reign of King Belshazzar a vision appeared to me, Daniel, after the one that had appeared to me at first.
mismatches.sourceText: Vision of a Ram and a Goat 8

mismatches.activeText: In the vision I saw myself in Susa the capital, in the province of Elam, and I was by the river Ulai.
mismatches.sourceText: In the vision I was looking and saw myself in Susa the capital, in the province of Elam, and I was by the river Ulai.

mismatches.activeText: I saw the ram charging westward and northward and southward. All beasts were powerless to withstand it, and no one could rescue from its power; it did as it pleased and became strong.
mismatches.sourceText: I saw the ram charging westwards and northwards and southwards. All beasts were powerless to withstand it, and no one could rescue from its power; it did as it pleased and became strong.

mismatches.activeText: As I was considering, a male goat came from the west across the face of the whole earth without touching the ground; the goat had a horn between its eyes.
mismatches.sourceText: As I was watching, a male goat appeared from the west, coming across the face of the whole earth without touching the ground. The goat had a horn between its eyes.

mismatches.activeText: It came toward the ram with the two horns that I had seen standing beside the river, and it ran at it with savage force.
mismatches.sourceText: It came towards the ram with the two horns that I had seen standing beside the river, and it ran at it with savage force.

mismatches.activeText: Then the male goat grew exceedingly strong; but when it was most powerful, the great horn was broken, and in its place there came up four prominent horns toward the four winds of heaven.
mismatches.sourceText: Then the male goat grew exceedingly great; but at the height of its power, the great horn was broken, and in its place there came up four prominent horns towards the four winds of heaven.

mismatches.activeText: Out of one of them came another horn, a little one, which grew exceedingly great toward the south, toward the east, and toward the beautiful land.
mismatches.sourceText: Out of one of them came another horn, a little one, which grew exceedingly great towards the south, towards the east, and towards the beautiful land.

mismatches.activeText: Even against the prince of the host it acted arrogantly; it took the regular burnt offering away from him and overthrew the place of his sanctuary.
mismatches.sourceText: Even against the prince of the host it acted arrogantly; it took the regular burnt-offering away from him and overthrew the place of his sanctuary.

mismatches.activeText: Because of wickedness, the host was given over to it together with the regular burnt offering; it cast truth to the ground, and kept prospering in what it did.
mismatches.sourceText: Because of wickedness, the host was given over to it together with the regular burnt-offering; it cast truth to the ground, and kept prospering in what it did.

mismatches.activeText: Then I heard a holy one speaking, and another holy one said to the one that was speaking, “For how long is this vision concerning the regular burnt offering, the transgression that makes desolate, and the giving over of the sanctuary and host to be trampled?”
mismatches.sourceText: Then I heard a holy one speaking, and another holy one said to the one that spoke, ‘For how long is this vision concerning the regular burnt-offering, the transgression that makes desolate, and the giving over of the sanctuary and host to be trampled?’

mismatches.activeText: And he answered him, “For two thousand three hundred evenings and mornings; then the sanctuary shall be restored to its rightful state.”
mismatches.sourceText: And he answered him, ‘For two thousand three hundred evenings and mornings; then the sanctuary shall be restored to its rightful state.’

mismatches.activeText: When I, Daniel, had seen the vision, I tried to understand it. Then someone appeared standing before me, having the appearance of a man,

mismatches.activeText: and I heard a human voice from the Ulai, calling out, “Gabriel, help this man understand the vision.”

mismatches.activeText: So he came near where I stood; and when he came, I was terrified and fell prostrate. But he said to me, “Understand, O mortal, that the vision is for the time of the end.”
mismatches.sourceText: So he came near where I stood; and when he came, I became frightened and fell prostrate. But he said to me, ‘Understand, O mortal, that the vision is for the time of the end.’

mismatches.activeText: As he was speaking to me, I fell into a deep sleep with my face to the ground; but he touched me and set me on my feet.
mismatches.sourceText: As he was speaking to me, I fell into a trance, face to the ground; then he touched me and set me on my feet.

mismatches.activeText: He said, “Listen, and I will tell you what will take place later in the period of wrath; for it refers to the appointed time of the end.
mismatches.sourceText: He said, ‘Listen, and I will tell you what will take place later in the period of wrath; for it refers to the appointed time of the end.

mismatches.activeText: At the end of their rule, when the transgressions have reached their full measure, a king of bold countenance shall arise, skilled in intrigues.
mismatches.sourceText: At the end of their rule, when the transgressions have reached their full measure, a king of bold countenance shall arise, skilled in intrigue.

mismatches.activeText: His power shall be great, he shall cause fearful destruction, and shall succeed in what he does. He shall destroy the powerful and the holy people.
mismatches.sourceText: He shall grow strong in power, shall cause fearful destruction, and shall succeed in what he does. He shall destroy the powerful and the people of the holy ones.

mismatches.activeText: By his cunning he shall make deceit prosper under his hand, and in his own mind he shall be proud. Without warning he shall destroy many and shall even rise up against the Prince of princes. But he shall be broken, and not by human hands.
mismatches.sourceText: By his cunning he shall make deceit prosper under his hand, and in his own mind he shall be great. Without warning he shall destroy many and shall even rise up against the Prince of princes. But he shall be broken, and not by human hands.

mismatches.activeText: The vision of the evenings and the mornings that has been told is true. As for you, seal up the vision, for it refers to many days from now.”

mismatches.activeText: “Righteousness is on your side, O Lord, but open shame, as at this day, falls on us, the people of Judah, the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and all Israel, those who are near and those who are far away, in all the lands to which you have driven them, because of the treachery that they have committed against you.
mismatches.sourceText: ‘Righteousness is on your side, O Lord, but open shame, as at this day, falls on us, the people of Judah, the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and all Israel, those who are near and those who are far away, in all the lands to which you have driven them, because of the treachery that they have committed against you.

mismatches.activeText: Open shame, O Lord, falls on us, our kings, our officials, and our ancestors, because we have sinned against you.

mismatches.activeText: and have not obeyed the voice of the Lord our God by following his laws, which he set before us by his servants the prophets.
mismatches.sourceText: and have not obeyed the voice of the Lord

mismatches.activeText: “All Israel has transgressed your law and turned aside, refusing to obey your voice. So the curse and the oath written in the law of Moses, the servant of God, have been poured out upon us, because we have sinned against him.
mismatches.sourceText: ‘All Israel has transgressed your law and turned aside, refusing to obey your voice. So the curse and the oath written in the law of Moses, the servant of God, have been poured out upon us, because we have sinned against you.

mismatches.activeText: Just as it is written in the law of Moses, all this calamity has come upon us. We did not entreat the favor of the Lord our God, turning from our iniquities and reflecting on his fidelity.
mismatches.sourceText: Just as it is written in the law of Moses, all this calamity has come upon us. We did not entreat the favour of the Lord

mismatches.activeText: So the Lord kept watch over this calamity until he brought it upon us. Indeed, the Lord our God is right in all that he has done; for we have not obeyed his voice.
mismatches.sourceText: So the Lord

mismatches.activeText: “And now, O Lord our God, who brought your people out of the land of Egypt with a mighty hand and made your name renowned even to this day—we have sinned, we have done wickedly.
mismatches.sourceText: ‘And now, O Lord our God, who brought your people out of the land of Egypt with a mighty hand and made your name renowned even to this day—we have sinned, we have done wickedly.

mismatches.activeText: O Lord, in view of all your righteous acts, let your anger and wrath, we pray, turn away from your city Jerusalem, your holy mountain; because of our sins and the iniquities of our ancestors, Jerusalem and your people have become a disgrace among all our neighbors.
mismatches.sourceText: O Lord, in view of all your righteous acts, let your anger and wrath, we pray, turn away from your city Jerusalem, your holy mountain; because of our sins and the iniquities of our ancestors, Jerusalem and your people have become a disgrace among all our neighbours.

mismatches.activeText: O Lord, hear; O Lord, forgive; O Lord, listen and act and do not delay! For your own sake, O my God, because your city and your people bear your name.”
mismatches.sourceText: O Lord, hear; O Lord, forgive; O Lord, listen and act and do not delay! For your own sake, O my God, because your city and your people bear your name!’

mismatches.activeText: While I was speaking, and was praying and confessing my sin and the sin of my people Israel, and presenting my supplication before the Lord my God on behalf of the holy mountain of my God—

mismatches.activeText: He came and said to me, “Daniel, I have now come out to give you wisdom and understanding.
mismatches.sourceText: He came and said to me, ‘Daniel, I have now come out to give you wisdom and understanding.

mismatches.activeText: “Seventy weeks are decreed for your people and your holy city: to finish the transgression, to put an end to sin, and to atone for iniquity, to bring in everlasting righteousness, to seal both vision and prophet, and to anoint a most holy place.
mismatches.sourceText: ‘Seventy weeks are decreed for your people and your holy city: to finish the transgression, to put an end to sin, and to atone for iniquity, to bring in everlasting righteousness, to seal both vision and prophet, and to anoint a most holy place.

mismatches.activeText: He shall make a strong covenant with many for one week, and for half of the week he shall make sacrifice and offering cease; and in their place shall be an abomination that desolates, until the decreed end is poured out upon the desolator.”
mismatches.sourceText: He shall make a strong covenant with many for one week, and for half of the week he shall make sacrifice and offering cease; and in their place shall be an abomination that desolates, until the decreed end is poured out upon the desolator.’

mismatches.activeText: In the third year of King Cyrus of Persia a word was revealed to Daniel, who was named Belteshazzar. The word was true, and it concerned a great conflict. He understood the word and had insight into the vision.

mismatches.activeText: On the twenty-fourth day of the first month, as I was standing on the bank of the great river, that is, the Tigris,

mismatches.sourceText: Then I heard the sound of his words; and when I heard the sound of his words, I fell into a trance, face to the ground.

mismatches.activeText: He said to me, “Daniel, greatly beloved, pay attention to the words that I am going to speak to you. Stand on your feet, for I have now been sent to you.” When he had spoken this word to me, I stood up trembling.
mismatches.sourceText: He said to me, ‘Daniel, greatly beloved, pay attention to the words that I am going to speak to you. Stand on your feet, for I have now been sent to you.’ So while he was speaking this word to me, I stood up trembling.

mismatches.activeText: He said to me, “Do not fear, Daniel, for from the first day that you set your mind to gain understanding and to humble yourself before your God, your words have been heard, and I have come because of your words.
mismatches.sourceText: He said to me, ‘Do not fear, Daniel, for from the first day that you set your mind to gain understanding and to humble yourself before your God, your words have been heard, and I have come because of your words.

mismatches.activeText: But the prince of the kingdom of Persia opposed me twenty-one days. So Michael, one of the chief princes, came to help me, and I left him there with the prince of the kingdom of Persia,

mismatches.activeText: Then one in human form touched my lips, and I opened my mouth to speak, and said to the one who stood before me, “My lord, because of the vision such pains have come upon me that I retain no strength.
mismatches.sourceText: Then one in human form touched my lips, and I opened my mouth to speak, and said to the one who stood before me, ‘My lord, because of the vision such pains have come upon me that I retain no strength.

mismatches.activeText: How can my lord’s servant speak with my lord? For now no strength remains in me, and no breath is left in me.”
mismatches.sourceText: How can my lord’s servant talk with my lord? For I am shaking, no strength remains in me, and no breath is left in me.’

mismatches.activeText: He said, “Do not fear, greatly beloved, you are safe. Be strong and courageous!” When he spoke to me, I was strengthened and said, “Let my lord speak, for you have strengthened me.”
mismatches.sourceText: He said, ‘Do not fear, greatly beloved, you are safe. Be strong and courageous!’ When he spoke to me, I was strengthened and said, ‘Let my lord speak, for you have strengthened me.’

mismatches.activeText: Then he said, “Do you know why I have come to you? Now I must return to fight against the prince of Persia, and when I am through with him, the prince of Greece will come.
mismatches.sourceText: Then he said, ‘Do you know why I have come to you? Now I must return to fight against the prince of Persia, and when I am through with him, the prince of Greece will come.

mismatches.activeText: “As for me, in the first year of Darius the Mede, I stood up to support and strengthen him.

“Now I will announce the truth to you. Three more kings shall arise in Persia. The fourth shall be far richer than all of them, and when he has become strong through his riches, he shall stir up all against the kingdom of Greece.
mismatches.sourceText: ‘Now I will announce the truth to you. Three more kings shall arise in Persia. The fourth shall be far richer than all of them, and when he has become strong through his riches, he shall stir up all against the kingdom of Greece.

mismatches.activeText: Then a warrior king shall arise, who shall rule with great dominion and shall do as he pleases.
mismatches.sourceText: Then a warrior king shall arise, who shall rule with great dominion and take action as he pleases.

mismatches.activeText: But as soon as he has risen to power, his kingdom shall be broken and divided toward the four winds of heaven, but not to his posterity, nor according to the dominion with which he ruled; for his kingdom shall be uprooted and go to others besides these.
mismatches.sourceText: And while still rising in power, his kingdom shall be broken and divided towards the four winds of heaven, but not to his posterity, nor according to the dominion with which he ruled; for his kingdom shall be uprooted and go to others besides these.

mismatches.activeText: “Then the king of the south shall grow strong, but one of his officers shall grow stronger than he and shall rule a dominion greater than his own.

mismatches.activeText: With the passing of some years they shall make an alliance, and the daughter of the king of the south shall come to the king of the north to ratify the agreement. But she shall not retain her power, and his offspring shall not survive. She and those who brought her, as well as her child and her supporter, shall be given up at that time.

mismatches.activeText: “Then shall arise in his place one who shall send an officer through the glory of the kingdom to levy taxes; but within a few days he shall be broken, though not in anger or in battle.

mismatches.activeText: He shall stir up his power and determination against the king of the south with a great army, and the king of the south shall wage war with a much greater and stronger army. But he shall not succeed, for plots shall be devised against him.

mismatches.activeText: He shall pay no heed to the gods of his ancestors, or to the one beloved by women; he shall pay no heed to any other god, for he shall consider himself greater than all.
mismatches.sourceText: He shall pay no respect to the gods of his ancestors, or to the one beloved by women; he shall pay no respect to any other god, for he shall consider himself greater than all.

mismatches.activeText: He shall come into the beautiful land, and tens of thousands shall fall, but Edom and Moab and the main part of the Ammonites shall escape from his power.
mismatches.sourceText: He shall come into the beautiful land, and tens of thousands shall fall victim, but Edom and Moab and the main part of the Ammonites shall escape from his power.

mismatches.sourceText: He shall become ruler of the treasures of gold and of silver, and all the riches of Egypt; and the Libyans and the Ethiopians shall follow in his train.

mismatches.activeText: “At that time Michael, the great prince, the protector of your people, shall arise. There shall be a time of anguish, such as has never occurred since nations first came into existence. But at that time your people shall be delivered, everyone who is found written in the book.

mismatches.sourceText: From the time that the regular burnt-offering is taken away and the abomination that desolates is set up, there shall be one thousand two hundred and ninety days.

mismatches.activeText: Happy are those who persevere and attain the one thousand three hundred thirty-five days.
mismatches.sourceText: Happy are those who persevere and attain the thousand three hundred and thirty-five days.

mismatches.activeText: But you, go your way, and rest; you shall rise for your reward at the end of the days.”
mismatches.sourceText: But you, go your way, and rest; you shall rise for your reward at the end of the days.’
